findings.title: Missing Authentication and RBAC Implementation
findings.description: The task plan outlines the creation of numerous sensitive API endpoints (e.g., admin product creation, invoice management, crew scheduling) but completely omits any tasks for implementing user authentication (e.g., JWT, OAuth2) or Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). Without this, all endpoints are effectively public.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a dedicated task before or alongside API development to implement an Identity Provider (IdP) integration, define JWT validation middleware, and establish RBAC policies for all admin and internal endpoints.

findings.title: Insecure Storage of Infrastructure Credentials
findings.description: Task 1 specifies creating a 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap to store connection strings and credentials for PostgreSQL, Redis, and S3. ConfigMaps are stored in plaintext within etcd and are not designed for sensitive data, exposing the system to credential theft.
findings.recommendation: Modify Task 1 to use Kubernetes Secrets instead of ConfigMaps for all credentials. For production readiness, integrate a secret management solution like External Secrets Operator, HashiCorp Vault, or a cloud provider's KMS.

findings.title: Unrestricted File Uploads to S3
findings.description: Task 3 involves integrating S3/R2 for serving product images, implying an upload mechanism. There is no mention of validating file types, enforcing size limits, or sanitizing file names, which could lead to malicious file hosting or denial of service.
findings.recommendation: Update Task 3 to explicitly include strict input validation for image uploads, including MIME type checking, file size limits, and randomized renaming of uploaded files before storing them in S3/R2.

findings.title: Missing Pod Security Standards
findings.description: While network policies are planned, there is no mention of enforcing Pod Security Standards (PSS) or configuring secure securityContexts (e.g., running as non-root, dropping capabilities, read-only root filesystems) for the deployed services.
findings.recommendation: Add a production hardening task to enforce the 'Restricted' Pod Security Standard at the namespace level and ensure all deployment manifests include least-privilege securityContext configurations.

findings.title: Lack of Security Audit Logging
findings.description: Task 11 sets up excellent operational observability (OpenTelemetry, Jaeger), but there is no explicit requirement for security audit logging, particularly for the Finance service (Task 6) where tracking who created or modified an invoice is critical for compliance.
findings.recommendation: Update Task 6 and Task 11 to include structured audit logging for all state-mutating API calls, capturing the authenticated user ID, action, timestamp, and resource ID, and ensure these logs are securely aggregated.

summary: The current task plan establishes a solid functional baseline but critically lacks foundational security controls, particularly around authentication, authorization, and secret management. While production hardening tasks effectively address network security and operational observability, the application layer remains highly vulnerable to unauthorized access and data exposure. Immediate remediation is required to implement identity management and secure credential storage before proceeding with core API development.
strengths: Network policies are explicitly planned for production hardening to restrict pod-to-pod traffic.
TLS and cert-manager are included in the scaling tasks to ensure encrypted external transit.
Rate limiting is proactively implemented on public endpoints using Redis.
Dependency auditing (cargo audit) and static analysis (staticcheck) are integrated into the testing strategies.
